key_insight: A physics-informed neural network can learn transient elastodynamic wave propagation in bimaterial solids while enforcing governing equations, interface conditions, and dynamics directly in the loss.
engineering_relevance: The framework acts as a fast surrogate for split Hopkinson pressure bar and other high-rate solid mechanics problems, reducing dependence on repeated finite-element simulations.
method: Axisymmetric linear elasticity PDEs encoded into a PINN loss
Steel-aluminum bimaterial specimen used as the primary case study
ANSYS Explicit Dynamics results used for validation and training constraints
evidence_signal: The model closely matches finite-element predictions for wave reflection/transmission, displacement histories, face-averaged responses, and dominant stress/strain evolution, including unseen time instants and modified material properties.
watch_item: Performance still depends on the quality of physics constraints and training data, so extension to more complex geometries or strongly nonlinear behavior may require additional calibration.
paper_title: A Physics-Informed Neural Network Framework for Elastodynamic Wave Propagation in Bimaterial Systems
